data.title: Thermo Fisher slips after Q3 topline miss; guidance unchanged
data.description:
Thermo Fisher Scientific (NYSE:TMO) traded lower in the premarket on Wednesday as the Waltham, Massachusetts-based healthcare giant reaffirmed its 2024 revenue outlook after falling short of Street forecasts with its Q3 revenue.

A leader in the life sciences industry, Thermo Fisher (NYSE:TMO) reported $10.6B in revenue for the quarter, mostly unchanged from last year and missing the consensus by $30M as its main revenue generator, Laboratory Products & Biopharma Services segment, disappointed.

While revenue from Laboratory Products & Biopharma Services reached $5.7B during the quarter, unchanged from the prior-year period, Thermo’s (TMO) Life Sciences Solutions Segment added $2.4B to the topline, indicating ~2% YoY contraction.

However, TMO’s Analytical Instruments Segment brought $1.8B, and the Specialty Diagnostics Segment added $1.1B, implying ~3% YoY and ~4% YoY growth, respectively.

While its adj. gross margin slipped to 41.8% from 42.0% in the prior-year quarter and its adj EPS fell ~7% YoY to $5.28, Thermo Fisher (TMO) managed to beat Street forecasts with its earnings.

The company tightened its full-year adjusted EPS guidance to $21.35-$22.07, compared to its previous guidance of $21.29-$22.07, and reiterated its 2024 revenue outlook of $42.4B—$43.3B.

data.title: Stock index futures slip, yields tick higher as investors await Tesla earnings
data.description:
Stock index futures slipped on Wednesday, a day after the S&P 500 index posted back-to-back negative sessions for the first time since early September. Investors will now turn their attention to Tesla (TSLA) earnings.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -0.2%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -0.3% and Dow futures (INDU) -0.4%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 1 basis point to 4.23%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 2 basis points to 4.06%.

Wall Street ended in a mixed fashion on Tuesday, with the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite finishing higher.

"The markets are being weighed down by the recent increase in bond rates and declining hope on the direction of the interest rate," Patrick Munnelly, from Tickmill Group, said.

"With less than two weeks to go before the Nov. 5 election, investors are bracing for volatility in the markets," Munnelly added.

Investors will also keep their eyes on Tesla's (TSLA) Q3 earnings report on Wednesday, which will land after the closing bell.

On the economic front, the September existing home sales report will land during market hours, and is expected to come in at 3.88M.

"We think existing home sales were little changed last month, although a small decline is more likely than an increase," Pantheon Macroeconomics said.

The Fed's Beige Book will be released later in the day.

data.title: GR Silver Mining CFO Blaine Bailey to retire
data.description:
• GR Silver Mining (OTCQB:GRSLF) said its CFO Blaine Bailey will retire from the role, effective immediately.
  • The firm said it has appointed Robert Payment to the role of the finance chief.

data.title: Boeing in charts: Commercial Airplanes deliveries surge 10.5% Y/Y in Q3, while revenues fall 5.5%
Diversified Energy inks gas supply deal with Gulf Coast LNG facility
data.description:
Diversified Energy (OTCQX:DEC) on Wednesday announced a multiyear supply agreement with an unnamed "major Gulf Coast LNG facility" to provide natural gas to export.

Under the deal terms, Diversified (OTCQX:DEC) will provide ~40B cf of natural gas for three years starting in November under a fixed pricing construct indexed to Gulf Coast pricing.

The company also said it has taken advantage of the recent strength of the natural gas price curve to add to its hedge portfolio in 2025-27, with an average Nymex hedge price of ~$3.45/MMBtu.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Coca-Cola tops Q3 profit, revenue estimates
Microsoft co-founder Gates donated $50M to Harris to win election: report
data.description:
Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) co-founder Bill Gates has said privately that he has donated $50M to a nonprofit organization that is supporting Vice President Kamala Harris to win the 2024 U.S. presidential election, The New York Times reported.

The donation was supposed to be kept under wraps and was made to the Future Forward organization, which has become the main fundraising organization outside the official Harris campaign, the news outlet added, citing people briefed on the matter.

The donation went to Future Forward's non-profit arm, which is a tax-exempt 501(c)(4) organization and does not have to disclose its donors, The Times added.

While Gates has said he can work with either candidate, he has expressed concern what a second term for former President Trump would look like and is "significantly concerned" about potential cuts to family planning and global health programs if Trump were re-elected, some of the people added.

Gates is said to not have a "deep relationship" with Vice President Harris but has publicly congratulated the Biden administration for its work on climate change.

In a statement to the Times, Gates did not acknowledge the donation, but said this election is different, with "unprecedented significance."

“I support candidates who demonstrate a clear commitment to improving health care, reducing poverty and fighting climate change in the U.S. and around the world,” Gates told The New York Times. “I have a long history of working with leaders across the political spectrum, but this election is different, with unprecedented significance for Americans and the most vulnerable people around the world.”

Gates was a member of the board of directors for Microsoft and Berkshire Hathaway (BRK.A) (BRK.B) until he stepped down in 2020 to focus on his philanthropic efforts at the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. He stepped down from his role of CEO at Microsoft in 2000.

Microsoft did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.

Readers interested in investing topics tied to the upcoming election can find more information at the upcoming Seeking Alpha Investing Forum: Election 2024; please visit the event website.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Watsco misses Q3 estimates
data.description:
• Watsco press release (NYSE:WSO): Q3 GAAP EPS of $4.22 misses by $0.52.
  • Revenue of $2.16B misses by $80M.

data.title: Winnebago slides after earnings reflect sluggish retail demand
data.description:
Winnebago Industries (NYSE:WGO) reported a 6.5% decline in revenue in FQ4 to $720.9 million. The revenue decrease reflected the sluggish retail demand environment and operating inefficiencies within the Winnebago branded businesses. Revenue in the towable RV segment was down 7.2%, while revenue in the motorhome RV segment was down 3.1%.

CEO Michael Happe said the RV industry continues to face various headwinds, including uncertain retail conditions, higher inventory carrying costs and slightly elevated inventories in the motorhome segment, leading to continued dealer hesitancy and increased promotional efforts. "Despite the challenging operating conditions, in the fourth quarter we continued to focus on the variables within our control. We prioritized profitability and cash generation, aggressively managed inventory, and invested in new products and technologies to drive future growth," he highlighted.

Gross profit margin decreased 340 basis points during the quarter to 13.1%, reflecting higher warranty expense, operational challenges, and deleverage. Winnebago (WGO) saw an operating loss of $17.8 million vs. operating income of $57.5 million in FQ4 last year. Consolidate adjusted EBITDA was down 60% year-over-year to $28.7 million, vs. $48.9 million consensus. EPS came in at $0.28 vs. $1.41 a year ago.

Looking ahead, Winnebago Industries (WGO) anticipates total North American RV wholesale shipments in the range of 320,000 to 350,000 units. Based on that outlook and the current business environment, the company expects FY25 consolidated revenues in the range of $2.9 billion to $3.2 billion, reported earnings per diluted share of $2.40 to $3.90, and adjusted earnings per diluted share of $3.00 to $4.50. WGO's outlook considers prevailing trends in the RV sector, including competitive dynamics, shifts in consumer preferences, and key macroeconomic factors that may influence overall demand.

Shares of Winnebago (WGO) fell 7.78% in premarket trading on Wednesday. Recreation vehicle stocks in general are mixed in the premarket session as well, including Lazydays Holdings (GORV) +2.2%, Thor Industries (THO) -2.4%, LCI Industries (LCII) -0.2%, Camping World (CWH) -2.6%, Malibu Boats (MBUU) +0.1%, MasterCraft Boat Holdings (MCFT) -0.3%.

data.title: NextEra Energy Partners GAAP EPS of -$0.43, revenue of $319M misses by $22.17M
data.description:
• NextEra Energy Partners press release (NYSE:NEP): Q3 EPS of -$0.43 may not be comparable to $0.56.
  • Revenue of $319M (+3.6% Y/Y) misses by $22.17M.
  • NextEra Energy Partners continues to expect the run-rate contribution for adjusted EBITDA from itsforecasted portfolio at Dec. 31, 2024, to be in the range of $1.9 billion to $2.1 billion. The year-end 2024run-rate projection reflects expected calendar-year 2025 contributions from the forecasted portfolio atyear-end 2024.
